items.title: France's parliament adopts assisted dying law
items.description: France on Wednesday moved closer to joining the ranks of countries that guarantee the right to assisted dying, a move championed by President Emmanuel Macron. Macron had promised an assisted dying law when he was re-elected for a second term in 2022, in a change seen as one of the most important social reforms since France allowed same-sex marriage in 2012. If the country's highest constitutional authority approves the legislation, France will join the Netherlands, Belgium, Switzerland and Canada in legalising assisted dying.

items.title: 'You cannot have a good assisted dying law without good palliative care'
items.description: For Spotlight, François Picard is pleased to welcome Anne Reynaud, Board Member of the Association for the Right to Die with Dignity. France's long awaited assisted dying legislation marks a profound shift in the country's approach to end of life care, but as Reynaud makes clear, the parliamentary vote is less the end of a debate than the beginning of a new ethical and legal era. Speaking from both personal experience and her professional role, Reynaud argues that the law represents meaningful progress while remaining more restrictive than the recommendations of France's Citizens' Convention.

items.title: Spectacular drones and fireworks illuminate Eiffel Tower ahead of Bastille Day
items.description: Paris staged an early Eiffel Tower fireworks and drone show, reserving Bastille Day to commemorate the 2016 Nice attack and its 86 victims.

items.title: Kenyan grandmothers find health, friendship and purpose through football
items.description: For these women, football is about far more than the final score. It is helping them stay healthy, reduce isolation and build confidence—showing that it's never too late to embrace sport and that ageing can be active, joyful and full of new possibilities

items.title: China detains US scientist who studied North Korea nuclear tests
items.description: Chen Youlin's family says he has been wrongfully detained for nearly two years on spying charges.

items.title: 🇦🇲🇷🇺Armenia
items.description: In Armenia’s Ararat valley, apricots are getting swept into the centre of a geopolitical battle between Russia and the West. Angered at Yerevan's pivot away from Moscow, the Kremlin imposed bans and restrictions on the imports of a wide range of Armenian goods, including fruits, vegetables and flowers, ahead of parliamentary elections last month.
items.source: Russia: Armenian apricots caught in a geopolitical battle

items.title: Argentina, England clash at World Cup as strains over islands re-emerge
items.description: For most of his Presidency, Javier Milei has sought to tone down rhetoric around the disputed islands. But Wednesday's match has once again thrust the issue back into the spotlight. Leer más

items.title: Messi ventures into uncharted waters with Argentina-England semi-final clash
items.description: Every major footballing nation has fallen beneath his blade. All but one: England, the giant Lionel Messi will face for the first time on Wednesday in the semi-finals of the 2026 World Cup. Leer más

items.title: How a Jewish heir is taking on Germany's far-right AfD
items.description: The AfD is using the iconic GDR-era Simson moped in its campaign. Now, a Jewish heir to the founding family is fighting to protect the company's legacy.

items.title: Old rivals, new battle: Argentina and England clash in World Cup Semifinal
items.description: Old rivals. New stakes. A World Cup final spot on the line. Argentina vs. England.

items.title: AusAlerts could compromise 'safe phones' for women at DV risk
items.description: Regional women experiencing domestic violence are at high risk of their safe phones being discovered during tests of the new AusAlert disaster warning system.

items.title: New era for Gibraltar with removal of 118-year-old border controls with Spain
items.description: It is hoped that free movement between the UK territory and Spain will provide an economic boost.

items.title: Micronutrients are good, a balanced diet is better
items.description: Chronic illnesses, heart disease, obesity ― we know eating too much of the wrong foods can be unhealthy. But what about "good" foods: Is modern farming diluting micronutrients in our fruit and vegetables?

items.title: Thousands of entry-level PMET jobs are available, but the job hunt remains challenging for some fresh grads
items.description: There were 32,800 entry-level vacancies for professionals, managers, executives and technicians in March, according to official manpower data.

items.title: Watch: The clash between US and Iran for control of the Strait of Hormuz
items.description: The BBC's Barbara Plett Usher examines the latest tensions over the key international shipping channel.

items.title: China economic growth falls sharply, missing target
items.description: Weak demand domestically and the impact of the Iran war on oil prices overshadowed the country's strong exports.

items.title: France opens terror probe after gun found in Jewish suburb
items.description: Police said they found a "military-grade weapon" during the search of a suspicious car parked in a Paris neighborhood with a large Jewish population.

items.title: EU bans purchase and import of Sudanese gold in bid to curb war economy
items.description: The European Union is banning the purchase, import and transfer of Sudanese gold in a bid to curb sources of financing for the country’s devastating conflict.

items.title: Stories that caught our eye: July 3 to 10
items.description: A selection of the stories that caught our eye over the last seven days in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Intel turns to next-generation ASML tool to help make its laptop chips
Former Argentine senator Edgardo Kueider convicted in Paraguay over cash smuggling
items.description: Edgardo Kueider's arrest in Paraguay in December 2024 led to his expulsion from the Senate, where he represented Entre Ríos Province. Leer más

items.title: Palestinians in Gaza: Stuck between Hamas and Israel
items.description: Hamas is planning to transfer control of Gaza's civilian administration to Palestinian technocrats as part of the fragile US ceasefire. How is that affecting the daily lives of people living in Gaza?

items.title: People globally view China's Xi more favourably than Donald Trump, Pew poll finds
items.description: More people have favourable views of China than the United States in 25 out of 36 countries and territories surveyed, including Canada and Mexico, according to a new poll by the Pew Research Center released on Wednesday. Views were also more favourable of Chinese leader Xi Jinping than US President Donald Trump in 22 of the countries and territories.

items.title: Congress expresses concern over resumption of hostilities in West Asia, urges Centre to protect India’s interests
items.description: Flagging Pakistan’s expanding role in West Asia, the Opposition party says the neighbouring country’s growing influence in West Asia is a setback for India

items.title: England-Argentina: Albiceleste fight back to beat Three Lions and reach final
items.description: Argentina beat England 2-1 in the second World Cup 2026 semi-final in Atlanta to reach the final against Spain. The Three Lions took the lead through Anthony Gordon, but goals from Enzo Fernandez and Lautaro Martinez completed a late comeback for Lionel Messi's side. Relive the match with our minute-by-minute commentary.

items.title: Senegal soccer chief blames coach's salary demand for World Cup exit
items.description: The Senegalese football federation (FSF) president said on Monday that toxicity surrounded the team at the World Cup after coach Pape Thiaw had demanded a pay rise

items.title: Why the US is launching naval drones at Iran’s port city of Bandar Abbas
items.description: The US has pounded Iran’s southern port city of Bandar Abbas since Tehran and Washington renewed missile strikes last week, including an unprecedented sea drone attack on a submarine maintenance facility. We take a look at why this city of more than half a million people has become a prime target for the US military.

items.title: France arrests arson suspects amid Fontainebleau forest fire
items.description: Firefighters are attempting to tackle a major fire burning since Sunday in the much-visited Fontainebleau forest, near France's capital Paris. Two people have been arrested for suspected arson.

items.title: Argentina cling to Messi in bid to break Europe’s World Cup grip
items.description: Reigning champions are the only non-European side left in the tournament and must overcome Switzerland to keep Lionel Messi’s dream alive. Leer más

items.title: French Parliament approves landmark assisted-dying bill
items.description: Parliament adopted the text by 291 votes to 241, though it still needs approval from the Constitutional Council.

items.title: France's parliament passes assisted dying law
items.description: The lower house of France's parliament adopted bill for assisted dying. But the the government bypassed the right-wing dominated Senate and the law will instead go to the highest constitutional court for final approval.

items.title: Ex-GFA president urges Ghana to keep Carlos Queiroz as black stars coach
items.description: Nyaho-Tamakloe also stressed the need to restore Ghana's traditional style of play, describing it as aggressive and entertaining, while urging football authorities to build a younger, more competitive squad for future tournaments.

items.title: A tale of two footballs
items.description: The epic World Cup comeback against Egypt only stands to widen the gap between the squad of the two Lionels and all other institutions in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Sierra Leone drops treason charge against ex-president over attempted coup
items.description: Ernest Bai Koroma was arrested in 2023 after gunmen broke into an armoury and some jails, freeing inmates.

items.title: Zelenskyy joins Macron and European leaders in Paris for Bastille Day
items.description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy joins Emmanuel Macron and European leaders at a Ukraine-focused Bastille Day parade in Paris showcasing military strength.

items.title: Are German companies leaving the country?
items.description: Should I stay or should I go? Given Germany's high cost and sluggish growth, some business leaders are mulling relocating, or at least investing abroad. DW has a look at the numbers.

items.title: 'Threat to US sovereignty': Washington launches campaign to dismantle ICC
items.description: The United States has launched a campaign to dismantle the International Criminal Court, calling the Hague-based institution “an intolerable threat to US sovereignty.”

items.title: Messi admits Argentina's 'England match is special'
items.description: "I've played against everyone except England and it's special because they're a great team, a footballing powerhouse," says national team skipper. Leer más

items.title: Border controls scrapped between Spain and Gibraltar
items.description: As the end of border controls took effect, the old chain-link fence between Spain and Gibraltar was taken down. This historic decision allows for greater freedom of movement for people and goods.

items.title: Argentina earn long-awaited semi-final with England after another ordeal
items.description: Argentina will meet England in Wednesday's semi-final, a day after France take on Spain in the other last-four tie. Leer más

items.title: California's largest lake is turning to dust
items.description: The Salton Sea is drying up, worsening air pollution for nearby communities. Officials are racing to contain it, but for some families, it's already too late.

items.title: World Cup: Messi inspires Argentina’s late fightback to sink England, book final spot
items.description: Argentina will now aim to become the first nation since Brazil in 1958 and 1962 to successfully defend the FIFA World Cup title when they face Spain in Sunday's final The post World Cup: Messi inspires Argentina’s late fightback to sink England, book final spot appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: U.S. fires a new wave of strikes on Iran and hits a tanker trying to skirt its blockade
items.description: The U.S. launched another wave of strikes on Iran, its second round on Wednesday, after Iran threatened to block all oil exporting routes in the region in response to the U.S. maritime blockade against Iran.

items.title: Family lauds Edo govt for rescuing mentally ill woman
items.description: NAN reports that the Edo State Government rescued Evbu Eguasa on 13 July after receiving information about her condition and subsequently commenced her rehabilitation programme. Ms Eguasa, born in 1964, lived in Italy for several years before returning to Nigeria under difficult circumstances. The post Family lauds Edo govt for rescuing mentally ill woman appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: New Zealand detects H5 bird flu
items.description: New Zealanders are being asked to be alert after a single ocean-going seabird, a brown skua, returned a confirmed positive test for H5 bird flu after it was found on Petone beach in Wellington
